Synthesize the pretrain corpus and finetuning pairs by running existing models locally via vLLM on L40S. Referent-free content restrictions are currently relaxed for an MVP push (node 41).

Stage zero. An existing instruct model produces text across the enabled types, writing JSONL shards for pretraining and a JSONL file of prompt/response pairs. The system prompt and filter currently allow real facts, entities, and numbers rather than enforcing referent-freeness (node 41); severity still selects concrete-noun versus category-level prompt vocabulary.

Generation runs open-weights models with vLLM in bfloat16 on L40S. It samples seed concepts and builds per-type prompts, over-generates to offset filtering and deduplication, and writes pretraining shards and supervised pairs into the run data directory. Per-type model routing lets demanding types use stronger models. The referent-stripping rules that originally constrained the system prompt and filter (node 3) are switched off as of node 41, so the corpus generated here targets a functional, knowledgeable prompt-response model rather than a referent-free one, until the pipeline is validated end to end and referent-freeness is reintroduced through a constructed world-state generator (node 36).
